description_long: This is a rustic wooden fence composed of three horizontal rails attached to four vertical posts. The rails are uneven and slightly curved, giving the fence a handcrafted, natural appearance. The vertical posts are thicker and have a rough, cylindrical shape, contrasting with the flatter rails. The wood grain is visible, and the color varies between a lighter beige for the rails and a darker brown for the posts. There are no decorative elements or caps on the posts, and the construction appears simple and functional. This type of fence is commonly used to demarcate boundaries in parks, gardens, or along sidewalks in urban and suburban settings.
description: A rustic wooden fence with three uneven horizontal rails and four sturdy vertical posts, featuring a natural, handcrafted look with visible wood grain.
description_view_0: Front view showing three light-colored, uneven rails attached to four darker vertical posts.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the alignment of the rails and posts.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the darker wood texture of the posts and rails, with the same three-rail structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the arrangement of rails and posts.
